title: Adversarial Design Review — Cross-Cutting Findings (F-01 through F-25)
content: # Adversarial Design Review — Cross-Cutting Findings
> **Scope:** Identity Integration, Authentication, Action Portal
> **Method:** First-principles review validated against `~/nxgn.identity/main` source code
> **Status:** Mitigations integrated into module designs
## Finding Index
| # | Category | Finding | Severity | Status |
| F-01 | Security | M2M tenant context via X-Tenant-Id header | High | **Corrected** — Identity enforces via ServiceBindingMode |
| F-02 | Security | Permission cache 30-min staleness window | Medium | **Mitigated** — risk acceptance + configurable TTL |
| F-03 | Security | SecurityEpoch fail-open on reads | Medium | **Documented** — risk acceptance with rationale |
| F-04 | Security | No CORS policy on API layer | Low | **Documented** — BFF-only access by design |
| F-05 | Security | User.IsActive has no enforcement | Medium | **Mitigated** — enforcement added to UserResolver |
| F-06 | Architecture | Auth-context endpoint vs PermissionEnforcer fail-closed | High | **Mitigated** — interaction clarified |
| F-07 | Architecture | Bootstrap circular dependency with Identity | Medium | **Mitigated** — retry/backoff strategy added |
| F-08 | Architecture | Dual auto-create (tenant + user) ordering | Medium | **Mitigated** — step isolation documented |
| F-09 | Architecture | Shell bootstrap duplicates auth context | Medium | **Mitigated** — single source of truth designated |
| F-10 | Architecture | Nav filtering is UX, not security boundary | Low | **Documented** — endpoint [HasPermission] is the boundary |
| F-11 | Failure Mode | Redis as single point of failure (compound) | High | **Mitigated** — compound failure mode analysis added |
| F-12 | Failure Mode | Identity unavailability cascade | High | **Mitigated** — degraded mode design added |
| F-13 | Failure Mode | Clock skew between BFF and API | Low | **Mitigated** — clock skew tolerance configured |
| F-14 | Failure Mode | Connection pool tenant bleed | Critical | **Mitigated** — test coverage strengthened |
| F-15 | Design Decision | IMemoryCache cross-pod inconsistency | Medium | **Documented** — trade-off analysis expanded |
| F-16 | Design Decision | Single combined bootstrap endpoint | Low | **Documented** — trade-off with rationale |
| F-17 | Design Decision | User entity owned by auth middleware | Low | **Documented** — ownership model clarified |
| F-18 | Design Decision | Hardcoded rate limits | Medium | **Mitigated** — made configurable via IOptions |
| F-19 | Integration | Permission namespace collision | Low | **Corrected** — Identity scopes by (ApplicationId, Code) |
| F-20 | Integration | Tenant switching — no coordinator | Medium | **Mitigated** — partial-failure handling added |
| F-21 | Integration | ENH-2 API key claim mapping still open | High | **Documented** — workaround designed as if permanent |
| F-22 | Integration | Language ID flow undocumented | Low | **Corrected** — Identity has language_id in JWT from session |
| F-23 | Testing | No adversarial/negative security tests | High | **Mitigated** — test cases added to all modules |
| F-24 | Testing | No performance tests for auto-create paths | Medium | **Mitigated** — concurrency test cases added |
| F-25 | Testing | No end-to-end tests spanning all three modules | Medium | **Mitigated** — cross-module test scenarios added |
## Detailed Findings
### F-01: M2M Tenant Context via X-Tenant-Id (CORRECTED)
**Original concern:** M2M callers assert any tenant via `X-Tenant-Id` header with no server-side authorization check.
**Identity source validation:** Identity has three `ServiceBindingMode` values:
- **`OrgBound`** — M2M client MUST provide `org_id` parameter at token request time. Identity validates org-app entitlement and bakes `tenant_id` into the JWT. No X-Tenant-Id header needed.
- **`PlatformLevel`** — M2M client exempt from org-app entitlement. No `tenant_id` in JWT. These are platform infrastructure clients (e.g., monitoring, migration tools).
- **`NotApplicable`** — Non-service clients (user-facing). Tenant context via user authentication.
**Source:** `TokenEndpoint.cs:214-238`, `OrgContextContract.cs`, `ClaimsPrincipalBuilder.cs:97-98`

**Correction:** For OrgBound M2M clients, tenant authorization is enforced at token issuance — the JWT `tenant_id` is authoritative. The `X-Tenant-Id` header is only needed for PlatformLevel clients, which should be restricted to internal infrastructure services.
**Design change:** Updated `tenant-context/api-surface.md` to differentiate OrgBound (JWT tenant_id) from PlatformLevel (X-Tenant-Id with allowlist restriction).
### F-02: Permission Cache 30-Minute Staleness Window (CONFIRMED)

**Identity source validation:** Identity uses next-login model. PRD NG4 explicitly states: "Permission change push events — permissions apply on next login; Identity has no mechanism to notify consuming applications of changes."
**Source:** `docs/prd/roles/prd.md:82`
**Risk:** Admin revokes permissions; user operates with stale cache for up to 30 minutes. For EHS compliance platform, this is a material risk.

**Mitigation:** Configurable TTL with documented risk acceptance. Default reduced to 5-minute sliding / 15-minute absolute for standard deployments. 30-minute absolute retained only for high-availability deployments where Identity latency is a concern.
### F-03: SecurityEpoch Fail-Open on Reads (CONFIRMED)

**Identity source validation:** `SecurityEpochMiddleware.cs:98-113` confirms: high-risk requests (POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH) fail-closed with 503; low-risk requests (GET/HEAD/OPTIONS) fail-open with warning log.
**Risk:** Revoked user can read sensitive data during Redis outage. For EHS platform, action details and compliance reports are confidential.

**Decision:** Risk accepted. Rationale: (1) Redis outages are rare and short with Aspire-managed Redis; (2) blocking all reads during Redis downtime creates a worse availability failure; (3) the SecurityEpoch middleware is configurable via `IsHighRisk` predicate — AMPS can override to fail-closed for specific sensitive GET endpoints if needed.
### F-05: User.IsActive Has No Enforcement (CONFIRMED)
**Design change:** UserResolver now checks `IsActive` after resolving the User record. If `IsActive == false`, the request is rejected with 403.
### F-06: Auth-Context vs PermissionEnforcer Fail-Closed Contradiction (CONFIRMED)

**The interaction:** PermissionEnforcer's rule is "empty permission set = fail-closed (403)." But `GET /api/v1/auth/context` has no `[HasPermission]` attribute, so first-time users (who have no permissions) need it to succeed.

**Clarification:** The PermissionEnforcer only enforces when the endpoint has `[HasPermission]` attributes. Endpoints without the attribute skip enforcement entirely. The "empty set = fail-closed" rule means: *if an endpoint requires permissions and the user has none, deny*. It does NOT mean *if the user has no permissions, deny all endpoints*.
**Design change:** Explicit documentation added to `permission-enforcement/api-surface.md` and `auth-context/api-surface.md`.
### F-07: Bootstrap Circular Dependency (CONFIRMED)
**The problem:** Identity Integration bootstrap needs an M2M token from Identity. If Identity is slow to start, the bootstrap fails, health checks fail, orchestrator restarts — death loop.

**Mitigation:** Bootstrap uses exponential backoff with jitter (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 30s cap) and a configurable maximum retry count (default: 10). Health check reports `Degraded` (not `Unhealthy`) during bootstrap retries to prevent orchestrator restarts. Bootstrap is non-blocking for read-only operations — the API can serve requests for existing tenants while bootstrap completes.
### F-08: Dual Auto-Create Ordering (CONFIRMED)
**The interaction:** TenantContextResolver (step a) auto-creates TenantConfiguration. UserResolver (step d) auto-creates User. Both can fail independently.

**Clarification:** Pipeline steps are sequential. If step (a) fails, steps (b-f) do not execute — the request returns 500/503. Auto-create failures are transient (DB unavailable) or impossible (valid tenant_id from JWT). The idempotent upsert pattern means concurrent requests for the same new tenant/user converge safely.
### F-09: Shell Bootstrap Duplicates Auth Context (CONFIRMED)

**Design change:** `GET /api/v1/shell/bootstrap` is designated as the **single source of truth** for frontend session state. It internally calls the same handler as `GET /api/v1/auth/context`. The `AuthStateService` is populated from the bootstrap response's `SessionContext` field — there is no separate auth-context call during bootstrap. `GET /api/v1/auth/context` exists independently for non-portal consumers (CLI, MCP) and for mid-session refresh (tenant switch).
### F-11: Redis Compound Failure Mode (CONFIRMED)
**Compound failure when Redis goes down:**
| Component | Redis Role | Failure Behaviour |
| BFF Sessions | Session storage | New logins fail. Existing sessions: read fails → user re-authenticates on next request |
| SecurityEpoch | Epoch cache | Reads fail-open (revoked tokens honoured). Mutations fail-closed (503) |
| Rate Limiting | Counter storage (in-memory for AMPS) | No impact — AMPS rate limits are in-memory |
**Net effect:** Users with valid, non-revoked Bearer tokens can continue reading data. No new sessions can be created. All mutations return 503. Password-reset revocations are not enforced on reads.
**Mitigation:** Aspire-managed Redis with health monitoring. Alert on Redis unavailability. Documented as acceptable degradation — total Redis failure is a P1 incident regardless.
### F-12: Identity Unavailability Cascade (CONFIRMED)
**Cascade timeline:**
| Time | Effect |
| T+0 | M2M token acquisition fails. New user login fails (OIDC). |
| T+0 | Permission resolution falls back to cache. Tenant switching fails. |
| T+5min | Permission cache entries begin expiring (sliding TTL). |
| T+15min | Most cached permissions expired (absolute TTL). Endpoints with [HasPermission] start returning 503. |
| T+15min | Auth-context endpoint returns 503 (can't resolve permissions). |
| T+15min | Frontend bootstrap fails for all users. New tabs/refreshes show error page. |
| T+15min+ | Only users with already-loaded SPAs and cached permissions can operate. |

**Mitigation:** (1) Stale-serve window extended to 15-min absolute for graceful degradation; (2) health check endpoint reports Identity connectivity; (3) auth-context returns a `degraded: true` flag when serving stale permissions so the frontend can display a warning banner; (4) documented as P1 incident — Identity is a hard dependency.
### F-14: Connection Pool Tenant Bleed (CONFIRMED)
**Risk:** If TenantConnectionInterceptor fails to fire on a reused pooled connection, RLS filters by the previous tenant's context — cross-tenant data leak.
**Identity source validation:** EF Core's `ConnectionOpeningAsync` interceptor fires on every connection acquisition from the pool, not just first open.

**Mitigation:** (1) TenantConnectionInterceptor uses `ConnectionOpeningAsync` (fires every time); (2) TenantTransactionInterceptor uses `SET LOCAL` (transaction-scoped, auto-reverts on rollback/commit); (3) added explicit integration test: "sequential requests for different tenants on same connection return correct tenant data."
### F-19: Permission Namespace Collision (CORRECTED)
**Original concern:** If another application registers `actions.read`, it collides with AMPS's `actions.read`.
**Identity source validation:** Permissions are scoped by `(ApplicationId, Code)` with a unique database index. Two applications can have the same code without collision — they are different records.
**Source:** `PermissionTypeConfiguration.cs` — `entity.HasIndex(e => new { e.ApplicationId, e.Code }).IsUnique()`
**No design change needed.** The permission registry design already specifies `ApplicationId` in the batch upsert. Noted in the design for clarity.
### F-22: Language ID Flow (CORRECTED)
**Original concern:** Language ID flow is "ghost-designed" — unclear how `language_id` gets into the JWT.

**Identity source validation:** `ClaimsPrincipalBuilder.cs:59-62` retrieves `LanguageId` from the Session entity and adds it as a JWT claim. Updated via `POST /api/v1/sessions/switch-language`. Claim destination is access token only (not ID token).
**Source:** `ClaimsPrincipalBuilder.cs`, `ClaimDestinationsService.cs:56`, `SwitchLanguageCommand.cs`
**No design change needed.** The authentication design already documents `language_id` as a JWT claim. Added a note referencing the Identity source for traceability.
## Identity Source Code References
Key files validated during this review:
| Area | File | Lines |
| M2M ServiceBindingMode | `NxGN.Identity.Api/Infrastructure/Authorization/OrgContextContract.cs` | Full |
| M2M token issuance | `NxGN.Identity.Api/Features/Token/TokenEndpoint.cs` | 214-238 |
| M2M principal builder | `NxGN.Identity.Api/Features/Shared/ClaimsPrincipalBuilder.cs` | 78-106 |
| Org-app entitlement | `NxGN.Identity.Api/Infrastructure/Authorization/OrgAppEnforcementService.cs` | Full |
| Permission scoping | `NxGN.Identity.Api/Database/Configurations/PermissionTypeConfiguration.cs` | Unique index |
| Effective permissions | `NxGN.Identity.Api/Features/Permissions/Shared/Queries/GetEffectivePermissionsQuery.cs` | 26-107 |
| SecurityEpoch middleware | `NxGN.Identity.Validation.SecurityEpoch/SecurityEpochMiddleware.cs` | 98-113 |
| BFF session store | `NxGN.Identity.Bff/Internal/Session/BffSessionStore.cs` | 81-85, 108-111 |
| BFF refresh endpoint | `NxGN.Identity.Bff/Internal/Endpoints/BffRefreshEndpoint.cs` | Full |
| Token refresh middleware | `NxGN.Identity.Bff/Middleware/TokenRefreshMiddleware.cs` | Full |
| Switch tenant command | `NxGN.Identity.Api/Features/Sessions/Shared/Commands/SwitchTenantCommand.cs` | 36-183 |
| Language in JWT | `NxGN.Identity.Api/Features/Shared/ClaimDestinationsService.cs` | 56 |
| IsM2MToken (Validation) | `NxGN.Identity.Validation/NxgnClaimsPrincipalExtensions.cs` | Extension method |
| IsM2MToken (API) | `NxGN.Identity.Api/Infrastructure/Authorization/ClaimExtractor.cs` | Static method |
| API key validation | `NxGN.Identity.Api/Features/ApiKeys/Validate/ApiKeyValidationService.cs` | Full |
| Rate limiting | `NxGN.Identity.Api/Infrastructure/Redis/RateLimitOptions.cs` | Full |
